About.Name: Jacques Ilunga
About.AboutText: Hi, I'm Jacques Ilunga. I'm a Junior at Seattle Pacific University studying General Engineering with a focus on Robotics and Data Analytics. I’ve always been drawn to how things work, and I spend most of my time at the intersection of building physical hardware and figuring out the data that makes it smart. I’m a big fan of the hands-on side of engineering—I'd much rather be in the lab prototyping a new circuit than just reading about it in a textbook. I thrive in collaborative spaces where I can bounce ideas off other people and solve complex problems as a team. When I’m not in the engineering lab, you can usually find me at the gym or out exploring new spots around Seattle. I’m currently looking for a Summer 2026 internship where I can get some real-world experience in robotics or aerospace and keep building my skills.

Experience.ExperienceText: I started my journey exploring the world of robotics through an electrical engineering lens. Currently, I'm focusing on balancing rigorous coursework with hands-on lab work to build a solid foundation for a career in aerospace and manufacturing.
Experience.EducationText: Currently a Junior at SPU, I'm diving deep into Robotics and Data Analytics. My favorite projects so far involve bridging the gap between theoretical math and physical circuit prototyping.
Experience.SkillsText: I'm most comfortable at the intersection of hardware and software—whether that’s analyzing data trends or soldering a new prototype on a breadboard.

Project1.Title: Sallen-Key Bandpass Filter
Project1.Desc: For this project, I designed and prototyped a dual-stage active bandpass filter targeting a 10kHz center frequency with a Quality Factor (Q) of 4. Using LTSpice for initial simulation and Bode plot analysis, I verified the frequency response before moving to a physical breadboard prototype. This project involved precise component selection—calculating specific resistor and capacitor values to maintain system stability and gain accuracy across both stages.
